added: Recall — persistent memory via Somadian graph store. Cross-session, cross-project knowledge.
Async agent comms — Soma reaches you on your phone when decisions are needed.
Content versioning — know when hub protocols update, diff before upgrading.
Boot sequence extraction — move hardcoded behavior into user-editable MAPs.
Child MLR writing — background children persist a Memory Lane Reflection on exit, so `harvest` returns the real reflection instead of a placeholder.
Statusline children-count + cost badge — at-a-glance visibility into background fleet and spend.
AppleScript terminal drivers (ghostty, iTerm, Terminal.app) — macOS-native background panes for users who want OS-level windows.

timeline.title: A cleaner exit
timeline.tagline: Leaving a session no longer scrambles your terminal.

fixed: Exiting Soma — whether from a 30-minute idle auto-shutdown or the /exit command — no longer leaves your shell typing garbage characters. The terminal is now restored cleanly on the way out.

timeline.title: Behavior you own
timeline.tagline: Soma's behavioral rules move into a file you tune — like soul and voice.

added: soma:browser.render — pull clean, readable text from a JavaScript-heavy page in a single call, no running bridge required. Great for research and scraping where a plain fetch returns only an empty shell.
changed: All of Soma's behavioral rules now live in core_rules.md — a file you own and tune, like soul and voice. Your edits are preserved; an upgrade merges in what's new without overwriting them.
fixed: Browser automation survives a bridge restart — evaluate and navigate fall back to a direct connection when the bridge drops mid-task, instead of failing.
Background sub-agents on Claude now run reliably, drawing from your subscription.

timeline.title: Leaner, clearer sessions
timeline.tagline: A trimmer, reorganized prompt — your agent orients faster and spends fewer tokens.

added: A leaner, clearer always-loaded prompt — behavioral guidance reorganized into a simple Orient → Work → Remember loop, so the agent orients faster and spends fewer tokens every turn.
Sub-agents on Claude that can actually build, not just read — delegated children honor the tools their role declares, while still drawing from your subscription instead of metered usage.
changed: Lighter sessions — tool-loading hygiene trimmed a large slice of accidental cruft from every boot.
Sharper hand-offs between sessions — the next-session briefing gained Ground Truth, Open Loops, and Decisions Made sections, so your agent wastes less time re-deriving what it already knew.
fixed: The Edit tool no longer flattens a file's typography (em-dashes, smart quotes) on a fuzzy match — safe for prose-heavy work.
/inhale no longer replays stale code after a mid-session update, and the statusline preload badge reflects real state.

timeline.title: Ground before think
timeline.tagline: New Somas start with a sharper instinct — open the evidence before reasoning — plus a fixed startup crash and steadier edits.

added: **Ground before think.** New installs carry a sharper default instinct: open the file, the log, the running thing *before* reasoning about it — because reasoning that runs ahead of the evidence wanders into a plausible story. It's in the soul, the core rules, and how Soma briefs its next session.
changed: **Clearer delegation docs.** The guide now leads with the reliable way to run a background helper (`headless`) and explains when to use each mode.
fixed: **The TUI no longer crashes after `/inhale`.** A startup error could close the terminal right after loading a session — fixed, and the packaging flaw that let it ship is now caught *before* release.
**Edits stop failing on a common malformation.** When the model crams two changes into one (a frequent Opus quirk), the Edit tool now untangles it instead of rejecting the whole edit.

timeline.title: A tidier inbox
timeline.tagline: Soma marks letters read as it handles them — the startup inbox shows what's actually pending.

added: **Inbox read-status.** Soma marks letters read, actioned, or archived as it handles them — the unread list at startup reflects what genuinely needs attention rather than growing forever.
fixed: **Exhale no longer crashes in a fresh directory.** Ending a session in a folder that was not yet set up could abort the whole save; it now prepares the directory first.

timeline.title: Runtime refresh
timeline.tagline: Caught up with upstream Pi — steadier shell output, cleaner crash recovery, sturdier `soma init`.

fixed: **Latest Pi runtime.** Picks up upstream fixes — more reliable capture of output from fast-finishing shell commands, and cleaner terminal restore if a session ever crashes.
**Sturdier `soma init`.** Closed a dependency-resolution gap that could fail a fresh install on a clean machine.

timeline.title: Guard & settings hardening
timeline.tagline: Capable models skip the nags; the catastrophic prompts never relax — and the guards finally cover edits too.

added: **Per-model guard allowlist.** Trust sonnet/opus to skip the core-file + bash confirmations while weaker models and new users keep full protection — `guard.trustedModels`, settable per-project or globally.

**Catastrophe guards are always on.** Wiping a `.soma` workspace or `.git` history, `git init`, and destructive runtime/publish ops always confirm — capability relaxes routine prompts, never irreversible ones.
fixed: **One preload confirmation, not two.** Saving a preload now shows a single clear confirmation instead of a redundant double-notification.
**Commits work with no email set.** Fixed a hook bug that silently blocked every commit in any repo without a configured git identity.
**The core-file guard now covers edits**, not just writes — editing a runtime file or `settings.json` is gated like writing it.

timeline.title: Migration safety
timeline.tagline: Upgrades reach behind installs without ever clobbering your customized files.

fixed: **Behind installs actually upgrade.** `soma doctor --fix` now migrates a workspace that skipped versions (backfills settings + fast-forwards the version) instead of silently doing nothing.
**Your customizations are protected.** Template auto-update only touches pristine files now — a customized `_memory.md` (or any `_*.md` you've edited) is kept, never overwritten.

timeline.title: Headless Claude sub-agents + a self-healing prompt
timeline.tagline: Delegate to Claude on your subscription — and the agent's memory layer is back in every prompt.

added: **Headless Claude sub-agents.** Spawn a child agent through the official Claude CLI — it draws from your subscription, not metered extra usage. `soma claude "<task>"` or delegate `model: 'claude-cli/sonnet'`.
**Delegation is teachable.** Ask any Soma to delegate and its help explains the children pattern — durable specialist roles you author once that get smarter every run.
fixed: **Memory layer restored.** A path bug had silently dropped the whole harness (breath, memory, preloads, how-to-work) from the system prompt — fixed, with a smoke test to keep it there.
**Statusline reliability.** The preload-saved and runtime-branch indicators work again, plus a rare keepalive crash during session recovery is gone.

timeline.title: Model list fix
timeline.tagline: Listing your models works again, plus quieter dev ergonomics under the hood.

fixed: **`soma model --list` shows your models again.** A path was leaking into the command's search, so it always returned zero results; both `soma model --list` and bare `soma model` now behave correctly.

timeline.title: Runtime refresh
timeline.tagline: Soma moves to the latest runtime, inheriting a batch of upstream reliability fixes.

fixed: **Clearer API errors.** When a provider refuses a request, Soma now keeps the provider's own explanation instead of collapsing it into a generic error.
**Corrected model limits.** Context-window sizes for several models were fixed upstream, so requests no longer overflow a model's real limit.

timeline.title: Fable 5, and a cadence Soma runs on itself
timeline.tagline: Anthropic's frontier model lands first-class, plus a cadence that carries work across sessions.

added: **Claude Fable 5, first-class.** Anthropic's Mythos-class model — 1M context, vision, deep adaptive thinking — is selectable with `soma --model fable`, with cost tracked per token.

**The meta-workflow cadence.** How work flows across sessions and improves from its own incidents now ships as a core protocol you adopt per-project — just ask Soma to 'set up the meta-workflow cadence.'
fixed: **The preload indicator tells the truth.** The statusline no longer sticks on 'stale' after you write a fresh preload, and a prior session's file no longer shows a false 'saved' at boot.
**Idle sessions save before they close.** When keepalive runs out, Soma finishes writing your preload before shutting down — no more losing the handoff to a race.

timeline.title: Reliable image reading

fixed: **Reading images works reliably again** — screenshots, diagrams, and generated cards resize and load correctly (a packaging issue could silently disable image reads on some installs). `soma update` picks up the fix.

timeline.tagline: Show Soma a screenshot — it sees it.

timeline.title: Headless delegation — background sub-agents on free models

added: **Headless delegation.** Soma spawns background sub-agents on free models — off your subscription's usage limits — for cheap parallel grunt-work (scouting, summarizing, drafting) while your main session stays focused.
**Chain delegation.** String sub-agents into a pipeline — scout → planner → worker — where each step feeds the next, all on the minimal-inference path.
fixed: **`soma --help` now shows Soma's commands** even inside a project with custom extensions (it was falling through to the raw engine help).

timeline.tagline: Hand the busywork to free background agents — keep your main session sharp.

timeline.title: /body update command + doctor template suggestions

added: **`/body update` command — version-aware template updates.** Compares body files against latest templates, respects customizations, creates backups before any change.
**Doctor now suggests `/body update`.** Both the health-check and migration paths detect stale templates and point you at the guided update tool.

timeline.tagline: Body templates evolve — now with version-aware comparison and guided updates.

timeline.title: Tab Registry — stable browser IDs + body templates evolve

added: **Tab Registry — your browser tabs have stable names.** Every tab gets a UUID that survives CDP ID rotation. `tabs` returns friendly IDs, `navigate` returns them, `activate_tab` just works.
**core_rules.md ships with every new project.** Bookkeeping, Probe Before Reason, Search-Before-Build, Fix→Meta, Semantic Audit — the behavioral defaults we developed over months.
changed: **Body templates evolved across 6 files.** `body.md` grew a routing table + librarian directive. `soul.md` learned How I Learn/Build/Communicate. `_memory.md` dropped 32% weight — sharper, lighter.
fixed: **Preload fallback now matches the template.** The hardcoded fallback in `core/preload.ts` used old section names from months ago — now synced with the canonical `_memory.md` format.
**Roadmap staleness caught at ship time.** New `soma-roadmap-staleness-check.sh` compares CHANGELOG vs roadmap.json — wired into the release flow so the website never falls behind again.

timeline.tagline: Browser tabs get stable names, body templates sharpen, releases can't go stale.

timeline.title: System-core ships + statusline preload fix + budget caps

fixed: **system-core.md finally ships from the correct path.** The build was copying from the wrong source — the "Structure-aware before raw" guidance was compiled but never deployed. Now it lands in every session.
**Statusline detects manual exhale writes.** Polls the preload file on disk — no more stale SAVED-PREL state after you write notes directly.
**Body slots respect your token budget.** muscle_digests estimates are now capped by configured tokenBudget instead of assuming unlimited space.

timeline.tagline: What you build finally deploys — statusline wakes, budgets hold.

timeline.title: Browser drives the web — xray, click, fill, wait

added: **Xray and click — survey before you act, then click through.** Xray walks the DOM as a compact tree. Click sends real CDP mouse events that bypass React Aria isTrusted — SPAs just work.
**Fill and wait — interact with forms and handle hydration.** Fill injects values with proper input/change events. Wait polls until the target element appears after page load.
**Navigate returns a tab ID you can chain.** Capture it from the response and pass it to subsequent ops — no more guessing which tab you're in.
**Screenshots save to disk for instant viewing.** No more invisible base64 — they land in /tmp and the response includes the read tool command.
fixed: **Tab-not-found errors now give you actionable guidance** instead of raw CDP dumps. Pass `verbose:true` when you need the full diagnostic.
**The TUI no longer renders raw function source in the statusline.** A theme-color migration slipped a bare function reference into the branch display — fixed mid-release.

timeline.tagline: Survey, click, fill, wait — your agent drives the browser like you do.

timeline.title: Theme crash fix + migrations that never skip

fixed: **`/inhale --model` no longer crashes the TUI.** Theme colors that weren't in Pi's palette have been replaced — your header and statusline render cleanly.

**Migrations can't be skipped by version gaps.** Sentinel checks now run on every boot — even if you jump from v0.26.0 straight to v0.28.2, the settings backfill and template update still land on first launch.

timeline.tagline: No more theme crash on inhale — version-gap fixes land on first launch.

timeline.title: Preload fix + exhale notes + theme colors + release gates

fixed: **`soma inhale --model <model>` loads your preload.** The model name no longer steals the preload target — start fresh with any model and your full session context in one command.

**`/exhale <note>` directs the current agent during preload writing.** Text after `/exhale` is injected as a `### Note` in the EXHALE message. The agent uses it to scope the wrap (skip steps for "quick") and to pass directives forward to the next session's Start Here.
**TUI respects your theme.** Header and statusline now render through Pi's theme system instead of hardcoded escape codes. Colors match what you chose.
**Release can't half-ship anymore.** Main-sync is a hard gate — v0.28.0's "published but stale runtime" can never happen again.

timeline.tagline: Any model, full context — your note survives and releases ship complete.

timeline.title: Pi runtime major upgrade — 842+ models, namespace migration

added: **842+ models total.** Pi runtime jumped 5 versions (0.73.1 → 0.78.0). Together AI, OpenCode Go, Gemini 3.x, Claude Opus 4.8 — all available immediately without configuration.
**New namespace.** All four Pi packages migrated from @mariozechner/* to @earendil-works/*. Clean break from deprecated packages.

**`soma model

project` added.** Set per-project defaults. Your open-source work can run a free-tier model while paid projects use Opus — no manual switching.
**`soma inhale --model` flag forwarding.** Start a fresh session with a specific model while loading your preload. Both in one command.
fixed:
**`soma model

set` actually works now.** A pipeline-priority bug meant your model choice was silently ignored. Fixed — what you set is what loads.
**Two runtime patches retired.** Pi now handles terminal-title branding and content guards natively. Less code to maintain.

timeline.tagline: 842 models. One pipeline. Your choice actually sticks.

timeline.title: Preload lifecycle state machine + _dev consolidation

added: **Testing overhaul — faster, keyless, cleaner.** 117 sandbox tests run offline with simulated responses. Shared boilerplate, clean CI logs, trivial new-test setup.
**Preload lifecycle unified.** /exhale, auto-rotate, and the context safety net coordinate through one state model. No more competing rotation requests — every session saves cleanly.
**Three-way auto-breath.** Soma adapts: proactive, adaptive, or manual. Context management that learns your rhythm.
fixed: **Safety net respects your choices.** Manual /exhale and keepalive auto-exhale are no longer overridden.
**Preload always saves before rotation.** High-context cycling no longer skips your session state.

timeline.tagline: Every session saves cleanly. Soma adapts to how you work.

added: **Ancestor .soma/ walk-up.** Run `soma` from any subdirectory and your config, extensions, and preloads load correctly. No more cd'ing to the project root.
**OpenRouter session continuity.** `session_id` in request body so OpenRouter sees your sessions as one conversation. Session-aware providers get the full picture.
fixed: **Boot crash on extensions.** No more `Cannot find module` errors when loading `.soma/extensions`. Now scans for actual .js/.ts files.
**Statusline shows real model names.** “claude-sonnet-4” instead of opaque IDs. $0-cost models now read “Free.”

timeline.title: Soma finds its feet
timeline.tagline: Boots clean from anywhere. Shows its real name.

timeline.title: argv injection fix + code-cap hardening + OpenRouter sessions

added: **OpenRouter session grouping.** Every Soma session now passes `session_id` to OpenRouter's API, grouping multi-turn conversations on their dashboard for debugging and monitoring.
**Code-cap architecture hardening.** `soma:code.*` caps use async subprocess with 30s timeout, env-overridable script paths, and a build-time regression gate. No more event-loop blocking.
fixed: **CLI subcommand dispatch restored.** User-global resource paths (v0.27.3) unintentionally broke every CLI subcommand — `soma inhale`, `soma code`, `soma doctor`, all dead. Fixed by switching `argv.splice` to `argv.push`.
**Preload loading works again.** `soma inhale --list` now shows available preloads with ages. `soma inhale` loads the most recent.
**Two pre-existing test failures repaired.** `test-doctor.sh` and `test-soma-github-local-runtime.sh` now pass.

added: **Personal resource paths.** Drop extensions, skills, prompts, and themes at `~/.soma/{extensions,skills,prompts,themes}/` — separate from the runtime install. Your customizations survive Soma updates.

**Runtime safety guards.** Destructive operations on the Soma install now require explicit confirmation. Expensive ops (npm publish, docker push, remote rsync) prompt before running. Boot validates the runtime environment and prevents accidental downgrades.
fixed: Gitignore inline-comment patterns now work correctly — `sessions/` and `settings.json` no longer show as untracked.

timeline.title: Personal resource paths + runtime safety guards
timeline.tagline: Your extensions survive updates. The runtime protects itself.

timeline.title: Tool-list ergonomics — action-oriented cheat sheet

added: **`soma({op:'list'})` is now a cheat sheet.** Each cap family gets one line: `soma:code` use BEFORE `Read` for big files; `soma:github` use BEFORE planning OSS-overlapping work.
changed: **Docs caught up.** `whats-new.md` now reflects v0.25 / v0.26 / v0.27.x. New `system-prompt.md` section explains why `{{skills_block}}` isn't a duplicate. `extending.md` documents seven `soma-dev delegate` workflows.

timeline.tagline: Reach for the right tool on the first try.

timeline.title: Sonnet wall-aware breathe + autonomous PR ship

added: **Auto-breathe adapts to your model.** New tri-state `breathe.auto` with per-model thresholds. On Sonnet, warns at 28-33% and auto-exhales at 34-50% — before Anthropic's ~48% long-context wall.
changed: **No more double preload on `/inhale`.** Empirically verified across 10 sessions: every rotation produced two preload-injection messages. Single inject now; ~8K tokens saved per rotation.
**Honest auto-breathe text.** `Auto-breathe: rotating at N%` → `Preload requested at N% — rotating after agent writes it`. Says what's actually happening.
fixed: **Release-please is now the canonical ship pipeline.** Push conventional commits → release-please files PR → merge → tag + npm publish. First end-to-end autonomous PR landed v0.27.1.

timeline.tagline: Wall-aware Sonnet + the autonomous PR pipeline lands.

timeline.title: Pi 0.73.1 — and Soma now sees Pi updates in every prompt

added: **Pi update gap lives in your system prompt.** When `pi-coding-agent` gets a new release, the version-behind count appears in every session — no more discovering upstream is four versions ahead by accident.
changed: **Pi runtime: 0.72.1 → 0.73.1.** All four pi-* packages bumped together (the lockstep rule). All seven of Soma's runtime patches apply cleanly.
fixed: **Long-context wall doc honest about the threshold.** Earlier prose claimed Anthropic's long-context tier triggers at ~200K tokens; that didn't match actual behavior. Hedged with empirical observation — users probe their own account threshold via the `extra usage required` notice.

timeline.tagline: Pi gap visible in every session. The wall is real; now the docs say so.

timeline.title: Soft-recovery from Anthropic's extra-usage notice

added: **Quiet auto-continue when Anthropic flags an extra-usage notice on the first turn after `/inhale`.** New `extraUsageRecovery` setting (default `"auto"`). When the first API call after a session rotation comes back with an extra-usage notice, Soma surfaces a single info notice and continues — no manual keystroke needed. Three modes: `auto` (notify + continue), `notify` (notice only, you continue), `off` (silent). Strictly fenced to first-call-after-rotation; harmless on every other turn.

timeline.tagline: When Anthropic flags a notice, Soma reads it, surfaces it, and keeps going.

timeline.title: Two small fixes that close a quiet gap

fixed: **Any GitHub repo as a local codebase — actually works now.** The 8 local-mode caps (`local_path`, `local_find`, `local_blast`, etc.) shipped in v0.24.0 with the schema right but a bundling gap quietly held them at "registered, not runnable" on every install. v0.26.1 inlines the cache helper into the bundled tool so the runtime path resolves cleanly. The `tj/commander.js local find …` flow that the v0.24.0 roadmap promised now runs end-to-end.

**Release pipeline self-check no longer cries wolf during ship.** `test-release-completeness.sh` Section 4 (npm/package.json drift) now respects the same in-flight detection the rest of the suite uses — skips cleanly while a release is in motion instead of failing on transient state that resolves seconds later.

timeline.tagline: When a feature is shipped but quietly broken, the fix isn't drama — it's the line that says "and now it runs."

timeline.title: Custom tools just work. Terminals self-heal.

added: **Custom tool template fixed** — the canonical `_tool-template.ts` example was teaching a broken shape that quietly produced malformed records. Now it matches what Pi's runtime actually expects.

**Stale-preload pings you once** — when your preload drifts (5+ writes since save), you get a one-time `🟡 Preload now stale` notify, and a `🟢 Preload refreshed` after `/exhale`. No more every-turn green spam.
fixed: **Tool extensions can't crash sessions** — if a custom tool returns the wrong shape, Soma wraps it safely instead of letting the malformation cascade through the renderer and the cache.
**Terminals recover after a crash** — the TUI now restores terminal modes on exit, so your shell returns to normal instead of stuck emitting key-response sequences.
**Session restore works on `soma -c`** — sessions with malformed tool history reload cleanly instead of erroring on the input line.

timeline.tagline: Custom tools work. Stale preloads ping once. Crashed terminals self-heal.

timeline.title: Update on your schedule. No surprise stalls.

added: **Soma asks before updating** — when a new version is available, you get an interactive prompt at startup: `(c)ontinue` with what you have, `(u)pdate now`, or `(s)kip` this version. Skip persists until new commits arrive. The check is cached; zero network at boot.
changed: **Body migrations always run, idempotently** — sentinel-tracked migrations now apply on every boot regardless of project-vs-agent version state. New users on the current version still get safety migrations applied; veterans don't get re-applied work. Foundation for cleaner shipping going forward.

**Body template (`_mind.md`) cleaned** — three redundant interpolation slots (`{{protocol_summaries}}`, `{{muscle_digests}}`, `{{scripts_table}}`) stripped, since `compileFrontalCortex` prepends them automatically. Existing user edits backed up to `_mind.md.bak-v0.25.0` before migration.
fixed: **`/inhale` works again on Pi 0.71.0** — the inhale handler had redundant context calls that triggered Pi's expanded stale-context guard. Cleaned up; routed warnings through the route plumbing.

**Spurious deprecated-extensions warnings removed** — the inherited Pi-cruft check was nagging about `.soma/tools/`, `.soma/hooks/`, and `.soma/commands/` directories that aren't Soma conventions. Gone.
**Default mind-template fallback aligned with shipped template** — test environments and `/soma debug` now produce the same template as a fresh init (previously interpolated removed slots).

timeline.tagline: Update on your schedule. Migrations always run. Pi-cruft warnings retired.

timeline.title: Releases can't ship half-done anymore

fixed: **Releases now verify they actually completed** — v0.24.0 silently shipped to npm with the runtime worktree stuck at v0.23.0 because the dev→main pull failure was being swallowed. The release script now exits loudly if the runtime didn't actually advance to the new version.
**Web-scrape now creates the destination folder before writing** — quietly losing fetched docs because a parent directory was missing won't bite again.
added: **Two regression tests for the release pipeline itself** — the cap-bus surface (every shipped capability has a floor count) and release-completeness (CHANGELOG ↔ git tag ↔ dev ↔ main ↔ dist all line up). These run on every prepare; if a previous release was incomplete, the next one fails fast before any new bump.

timeline.tagline: When a fix is invisible, it ships incomplete. Now it's visible.

timeline.title: Any GitHub repo is a local codebase. Auto-breathe goes opt-in.

added: **Treat any GitHub repo like a local codebase** — fetch a tarball in 1–5 seconds, then run full symbol search, blast-radius analysis, and structure maps against it. No cloning required.
**Ask the agent what changed** — `soma:docs.whats_new` pulls the agent-facing changelog on demand. `soma:docs.guide` resolves any guide by name.
changed: **Auto-breathe is now opt-in** — proactive context rotation at 70% was busting the cached prompt. The 85% safety net still fires. Re-enable when ready.
fixed: **Security advisory cleared** — Anthropic SDK bumped past CVE-2026-41686. No action required — upgrade to v0.24.0.

timeline.tagline: GitHub repos become local codebases in 5 seconds. Auto-breathe goes opt-in. CVE cleared.

timeline.title: Stray files don't leak; cache levers documented

added: **`cache.retention: "long"`** — flip Anthropic cache TTL from 5 min to 1 hour for `soma -c` after coffee-break gaps. Schema shipped v0.21.0; behavior not yet validated. *(see [docs/configuration#cache](/docs/configuration#cache))*

**`breathe.auto: false`** — opt out of proactive rotation at 50/70% if it's busting your cache. The 85% safety net still fires. *(workaround pending root-cause — see [docs/configuration#auto-breathe](/docs/configuration#auto-breathe))*
**Voice switching** — pick a voice for your session and the say-cap routes through it. Voice persists across `/reload`.
**Filter children by role** — `soma:agent.list` accepts `{role: "researcher"}` and friends, so you can narrow your background fleet view to just the role you care about.
changed: **Release pipeline catches stray files** — logs, scratch files, or unreviewed edits sitting in the agent worktree won't leak into the published runtime. The pipeline halts before publishing if the tree isn't clean.

timeline.tagline: Release pipeline gets stricter; cache + auto-breathe levers documented honestly. Voice + child-role filter ship clean.

timeline.title: Audit cleanup + delegate UX polish

added: **Progressive teach for delegate** — bare `soma:agent.delegate` call returns help with available roles, models, recent children, and examples. Saves a tool round-trip when next-self doesn't remember the surface.
**Cache-TTL rollback on aborted/errored turns** (gated, default off) — preserves the cache window when a long tool call gets canceled. Opt-in via `keepalive.rollbackOnAbort: true`.
**`soma:new.child` cap** — scaffold a new child role from a template; matches the existing `soma:new.muscle` / `soma:new.protocol` pattern.
**Child registry filters** — `soma:agent.list` defaults to last 7 days, accepts `{active_only: true}` and `{cleanup: true}` for registry hygiene.
fixed: Delegate-core split into 5 modules (was a 1887-line monolith) for easier navigation and review.
13 `_dev` scripts now share a single `_lib/find-root.sh` helper instead of inlining the same logic.
Commit-msg lint phase added to the release prepare orchestrator — non-conventional commits are caught before they land in CHANGELOG.

timeline.tagline: Audit pass + delegate UX polish.

timeline.title: Namespaced meta-tools end-to-end + bridge CLI + init hardening

added: **soma bridge CLI** — start, stop, restart, status, logs, config, setup. First-class bridge daemon lifecycle that works standalone without Somaverse.
**Unified meta-tools** — `soma:agent.*` (delegate/children/harvest), `soma:focus.*`, `soma:new.*`, `soma:terminals.*`, `soma:docs.*`, `soma:body.*` — 40+ capabilities through a consistent namespace.
**Family-level discovery** — call any `soma:*` prefix bare to see what's available. Every meta-tool family has built-in help.
**Hash-based template refresh** — doctor auto-refreshes pristine body templates to current bundled versions; warns with diff path for customized files.
**Pi runtime 0.69.0** — 42 upstream commits including symlink session resolution, skills dedup, and prompt caching improvements.
fixed: Pairing flow hardened — secret in header, umask, curl --fail, device-key shape-check.
Agent version reads from dist manifest instead of package.json — no more stale version stamps.
TUI no longer crashes after /reload — stale-context guard on footer render.
Smart partial-state handling on soma init — no more bail on edge cases.

timeline.tagline: Namespaced meta-tools, bridge CLI, and init hardening — 40+ capabilities through a consistent namespace.

timeline.title: Same-cycle audit patches

added: **soma model-sync** — one command puts every project on the current model. Audits global + project + any crawled `.soma/` dirs and bumps them together. Bundled default is `claude-opus-4-7`.
fixed: Task text with shell characters (`$(…)`, backticks, apostrophes) now arrives at background children exactly as written. Previously a literal task string could be interpreted before the child saw it.
`soma model-sync` no longer chokes on model ids containing an apostrophe — the write path takes values through the environment instead of interpolating them into shell.

timeline.tagline: Audit-cycle patch: tmux quoting, model-sync injection fix, and the four-command post-update flow.

timeline.title: Children control panel + tmux baseline

added: **Background children work for every user** — tmux is the shipping baseline. `delegate(background:true)` spawns a child in a detached session; attach any time with `tmux attach -t soma-child-<id>` to watch it work.
**children tail / steer / kill / harvest** — the full control panel. Peek a child's pane without focusing it, inject a steering message, stop a stuck child, or read its reflection and clean up.
**soma terminals CLI** — pick your terminal driver. `soma terminals prefer tmux` saves your choice for every future delegation.
`/reload` greets you with `[Soma Boot — /reloaded]` so you know the reload took effect and any "needs /reload" work from last turn is live.
Background-delegation guide with install hints, the attach-on-demand pattern, and troubleshooting.
changed: Model aliases (`haiku`, `sonnet`, `opus`) resolve to full model ids before a child spawns, so children always launch with the model you asked for.
`children list` keeps itself honest — a closed pane flips to `completed` without needing an explicit `kill`.
fixed: `delegate(background:true)` now works end-to-end. It had been silently broken since Phase A shipped.
`/reload` on a session with changes shows the actual delta again, instead of reporting "no changes."
Tall screenshots pasted into the TUI no longer cover the conversation.
Compaction summaries no longer accumulate stray temp-file paths across turns.

timeline.tagline: Background delegation for every user — tmux as the shipping baseline, full control panel (tail/steer/kill/harvest).

timeline.title: Cache Economics + Discoverability + Self-Knowledge

added: **Recent Ancestry** — the preload auto-injects your last three session-log entries, so the next self sees continuity without paying the cached-prefix cost.
**soma tool** from the shell + **`capabilities()`** inside a session — two ways to see what tools the agent actually has available.
**soma new muscle** and **soma new protocol** — scaffold a new skill or rule in one command, frontmatter pre-filled.
**Background delegation**: `delegate(background:true)` spawns a child and returns immediately; `children list` shows status across sessions.
`soma children spawn / list / watch / tail / kill` available to all users — shell-side and in-session views share one registry.
changed: Cached prompt prefix shrank ~25% per boot. Agent Notes moved to `memory/notes/soma-log.md` where they're read, not carried in the cached context.
Migration system is data-driven — `## Doctor Actions` blocks in the migration map declare what doctor should backfill. New settings additions no longer need CLI changes.
Terminal tab title reads `σ — <session> — <cwd>`, so you can tell two Soma sessions apart at a glance.
fixed: Pi's preventive OAuth-billing warning no longer fires on boot. Real billing errors are still caught at runtime and shown with context.
13 settings keys backfilled idempotently for projects upgrading from any version since v0.11 — your customizations are preserved.
Five Pro scripts (`soma seam / refactor / scrape / github / browser`) work for every install during the v0.21.x beta.

timeline.tagline: Cache economics + discoverability + self-knowledge. ~25% smaller cached prefix, callable tool registry, one-command muscle scaffolding.

timeline.title: Cache Stickiness

changed: Iterating on your setup is free — `/reload` keeps your session's cached memory warm, so there's no dollar cost for rapid iteration.
Body edits apply on your terms — run `/rebuild` when you want them live this session, or skip it and they land naturally on your next start.
The restart banner became a quiet statusline tag that tells you exactly what each file change actually needs.
added: `soma update --yes` for scripted upgrades — the documented `npm install -g meetsoma@latest && soma update --yes` one-liner now truly works unattended.
CHANGELOG.md ships with every install — read what's new without leaving your terminal.

timeline.tagline: Cache stickiness: /reload keeps your session warm. No dollar cost for rapid iteration.

timeline.title: Tool registry + three new tools

added: **`context_status` tool** — the agent can ground runway decisions on real numbers instead of estimating. Returns `{percent, tokens, contextWindow}`.
**`file_outline` tool** — markdown/text headings with line numbers. 10-15× cheaper than reading the whole file when you just need orientation.
**`search` tool** (MVP) — unified search with pluggable backends. Default ripgrep over cwd; opt in to Brave web search by setting `BRAVE_API_KEY`.
**`_tools.md` configuration** in your body chain — disable bundled tools, override field defaults, declare custom tools. Project → parent → global, like the rest.
fixed: Restart alert is calmer — quiet statusline tag instead of a banner when an extension changes.

timeline.tagline: Tool registry + three new tools (`context_status`, `file_outline`, `search`); `_tools.md` opens the bundled set up to your control.

changed: Pi assembles the system prompt now — Soma writes SYSTEM.md and APPEND_SYSTEM.md and Pi auto-discovers them. Faster boots, cheaper child agents.
`soma check-updates` shows three layers — CLI, agent, workspace — with the fix command for any drift.
`soma doctor` quietly bumps stale workspace markers when no migration's actually needed.
added: Eight child roles now — auditor joined for post-release review.
Five built-in code-search tools: code_find, code_map, code_refs, code_structure, code_blast.
Three more roles: planner, doc_writer, reflector. Seven total.
Curator can auto-apply proposals in one command — no second pass to integrate findings.
Per-role scratchpads track pending amendments at a glance.
Three specialized roles to start: verifier, builder, curator.
Curator reads every child's MLR report and writes proposals back to body files.
`soma-dev children mlr / curate / apply` CLI for the loop.
Cost and token tracking per child run.
Roles declare a model chain (preferred + fallbacks) instead of a single hard-coded model.
Health checks plus cooldown so flaky models don't burn budget retrying.
`delegate` tool — Soma can spawn a child agent for bounded work.
Role files (`body/children/<role>.md`) define each child's persona, tools, and model.
Sandbox isolation for safe delegation testing.

changed: Pi runtime → 0.67.68 — better network retry, stable date format, tool-call error handling.
Release script keeps dev and main in sync. Main can no longer fall a release behind.
fixed: `soma update` and `check-updates` no longer crash on fresh installs.
Thin-CLI help covers the full command surface.
Fresh `npm install -g meetsoma` works again — was shipping broken tarballs.
changed: `soma init` sets up your project; `soma update` updates the runtime — they're separate commands now.
Pi runtime pinned to your Soma version. Doctor flags drift instead of silently shipping stale.
added: Statusline pings you when an update's available.
`soma model` command — switch models from CLI with fuzzy matching.
Claude Opus 4.7 with adaptive thinking.
Upstream audit tooling for tracking Pi version drift.
changed: Smarter prompt caching — tool changes no longer invalidate the system prompt.

fixed: Image budget auto-compact loop — guard against repeated compact triggers

timeline.title: Somaverse Edition

added: `soma login` — pair your agent with Somaverse through a cloud relay.
28 workspace + browser tools through the hub proxy.
Device-key auth for automatic hub mode.

timeline.tagline: Somaverse Edition — the visual workspace where AI agents live.

added: Image budget auto-compact for screenshot-heavy sessions
Configurable breathe thresholds for large-context models
Sandbox testing from dev/main/beta sources
fixed: Script root-finding across 10 dev scripts
Removed 3 dead settings
Script descriptions in system prompt no longer show "---" for scripts with YAML frontmatter
changed: Documentation updated with current versions and extension list across 6 pages

added: Smarter error messages — raw API errors now show clear, actionable guidance instead of JSON dumps.
Auth-aware billing — detects your plan type (subscription vs API key) and handles billing hiccups accordingly.
Auto-retry on transient errors — session startup issues resolve themselves without manual intervention.
Runtime integrity checks — catches version drift before it ships.
Cache health tracking — see cache performance in the statusline. Alerts on costly invalidations.
Idle session detection — auto-shutdown prevents runaway sessions.
changed: Major cost fix — stopped a $152/day cache bleed from image handling. Sessions are dramatically cheaper.

added: soul.md replaces SOMA.md — identity is discovered from your codebase, not configured.
Minimal first-run boot — new projects start lean, Soma learns through use.
soma session tool — strip images from session logs (16MB → 2.6MB), list and analyze sessions.
11 bundled scripts (was 6) — more tools out of the box.
Extension allowlist — control which extensions load, doctor reports unlisted ones.
changed: Intentional preloads — `soma inhale` loads memory on purpose, not automatically.

timeline.tagline: Doctor & Migration — projects upgrade cleanly across versions.

added: Soma themes — sky-blue accent, custom dark and light palettes.
soma github — scan GitHub repos without cloning: structure, map, deps, audit.
Keepalive auto-pause — stops pinging on API errors to prevent runaway sessions.
Docker sandbox — isolated E2E testing for pre-release verification.
Project health dashboard — soma-health.sh shows project state at a glance.
changed: Pi runtime updated to 0.66.1 — bug fixes, no breaking changes.
Scripts reorganized — dev scripts separated from bundled scripts.

added: Inbox system — agents can leave messages for each other across sessions.
Intentional preloads — new autoInject setting controls when memory loads.
7 new documentation pages — scripts, sessions, inbox, workspaces, and more.
Script drift detection — catches when installed scripts diverge from source.
changed: TL;DR replaces Digest — cleaner warm-tier format across all AMPS content.
Documentation accuracy overhaul — 30+ stale references fixed.

added: Auto-exhale on idle — session saves itself when keepalive runs out.
Keepalive limits — configurable max pings to prevent runaway sessions.
MAP system — workflow automation maps for repeatable dev processes.
changed: Doctor improvements — better migration detection and template comparison.
Warm content format unified — consistent TL;DR loading across protocols and muscles.

added: soma doctor — project health check with automatic migration for version upgrades.
soma status / soma health — infrastructure health at a glance.
soma --version and soma --help — proper CLI help system.
Migration phases — automatic settings and template updates across versions.
First-breath template — guides the agent's first session in a new project.
Warm AMPS — protocols and muscles load by temperature tier (hot/warm/cold).
6 new test suites — doctor, settings, protocols, muscles, identity, migrations.
changed: CLI routing overhaul — commands discover scripts automatically.
Starter content updated — better defaults for new projects.

added: soma --help rewrite — shows scripts, commands, and usage.
soma-theme.sh — switch terminal color themes.
changed: DNA.md rewrite — clearer project conventions template.

added: /inhale guard — warns when no preload exists or preload is stale.
5 new scripts — soma-seam, soma-reflect, soma-query, soma-focus, soma-plans.
soma-code v2.0 — blast radius analysis, TypeScript error context, improved refs.
Slash command usage hints for 10 commands.
changed: Pi runtime updated to 0.64.0 with new extension APIs.
Semver discipline — feature releases bump minor version.

added: /soma doctor — migration check with auto-run and confirmation.
Monorepo-aware first breath — detects and lists sub-projects.
Richer preload template — Weather, Who You Were, Orient From sections.
Body template scaffolding — section headers in body.md and voice.md.
changed: System prompt budget raised from 4,000 to 10,000 tokens.
Init UX — detects global vs parent .soma/, three-way prompt.

added: Init UX — prompt before scaffolding, parent .soma/ inheritance.
Pi upstream sync — 71 commits, built-in tools as extensions.
Protocol coaching voice — 14 protocols rewritten from spec to guidance.
Command provenance — /soma status shows which extension owns each command.
soma inhale — CLI preload management with --list and partial name match.
soma map — top-level MAP subcommand with --list and prompt config.
Settings-driven heat overrides — per-project AMPS heat control.
Statusline preload indicator — shows save status, smart /exhale detection.
Hub CLI — /hub install, /hub share, /list remote for community content.
Heat auto-detect — protocols and muscles automatically get temperature tiers.
soma-code.sh — codebase navigator with map, find, refs, replace.
Session export — /export saves conversation as styled HTML.
Hub integration — install protocols and muscles from the community.
Drop-in script commands — soma code, soma verify, soma refactor.
Core protocols bundled — breath-cycle, working-style, heat-tracking.
Heat system — protocols and muscles load by temperature (hot/warm/cold).
Automatic migration — settings and templates upgrade across versions.
Community sync — protocols shared between projects via hub.
npm install — meetsoma package published, one-command install.
Pi runtime 0.61.1 — stable upstream with extension support.
27 documentation pages — full coverage of features and configuration.
Open install — anyone can install via npm, no invite needed.
Voice system — agent communication style persists across sessions.
23 documentation pages — getting started, protocols, muscles, configuration.
Extension system — TypeScript hooks for custom agent behavior.
Scratch pad — persistent notes that survive across sessions.
Session warnings — alerts for context limits and idle detection.
Code scanning tools — soma-code.sh with structure and find commands.
Router extension — /soma commands for agent self-management.
Auto-breathe — automatic session rotation at context limits.
Dev tools — soma-verify, soma-refactor for codebase maintenance.
Prompt intelligence — system prompt compiles from identity + protocols + muscles.
Session stabilization — reliable session logs and preload handoffs.
AMPS architecture — protocols, muscles, automations, scripts as four layers.
Distribution system — bundled vs installable content tiers.
Session intelligence — preloads carry context across session boundaries.
Breath cycle — inhale, work, exhale rhythm for session management.
The engine — core agent runtime with extension loading and identity discovery.
Settings system — JSON configuration for behavior tuning.

timeline.title: First breath

added: σῶμα (sōma) — *Greek for "body."* Built on Pi with `piConfig.configDir: ".soma"`. Identity from `.soma/identity.md` — discovered, not configured.
Memory structure — `.soma/memory/` for muscles, sessions, preloads.
Breath cycle — sessions exhale what was learned; next session inhales it.

timeline.tagline: Soma's first breath — 9 core modules, 4 extensions, logo through 36 SVG iterations.
